verifier_values.criteria: States that Sweetmarch Build Partners can recover up to $216,340 from their insurance company for the fraudulent invoicing damages
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: Since the damages are subject to the $25,000 deductible (for each occurence), the total damages of $241,340 minus $25,000 is $216,340. This is the maximum recoverable because it is the amount that Western Contractors Risk & Indemnity Co. indemnified under the policy.

verifier_values.criteria: States that Western Contractors Risk & Indemnity Co. agreed to indemnify Sweetmarch Build Partners from financial losses arising out of fraudulent invoicing
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
Section 5.3 of the Independent Damages Report gives a detailed line item report of what the fraudulent invoicing entails. Each of these line items are directly covered under Section III.A. of the Subcontractor Performance, Integrity, and Misconduct Protection Policy (SPIMPP Insurance Policy).

See pages 1 and 2 of "Insurance Policy - Western Contractors Risk & Indemnity Co .docx":
Subject to all terms, conditions, limitations, and exclusions herein, WCRI agrees to indemnify Sweetmarch Build Partners (SBP) against Direct Loss, Resulting Damage, and Extra Expenses arising out of:
[...]
D. Falsified construction documentation, including but not limited to:
inspection reports,
weld certificates,
quality assurance logs,
invoices,
delivery receipts.

[...]

III. Coverage Sections

A. Subcontractor Fraud & Dishonesty Coverage

WCRI will indemnify SBP for financial loss resulting from:

1. Forgery or alteration of inspection documents, certificates, or compliance reports
including structural and weld certifications required for steel beam construction.

2. False or inflated invoicing
including billing for materials not delivered or labor not performed.

3. Intentional concealment of defects or unsafe conditions
such as falsified welding penetration reports or backdated QA/QC logs.

Limit of Liability (A): $5,000,000 aggregate per policy period
Deductible: $25,000 per occurrence

verifier_values.criteria: States that Western Contractors Risk & Indemnity Co.'s indemnification for Sweetmarch Build Partners' financial losses arising from fraudulent invoices are subject to a $25,000 deductible
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
Since these damages are covered under Section III.A. of the SPIMPP Insurance Policy, they are subject to that specific provisions deductible, which is stated as $25,000 per occurence.

See page 2 of "Insurance Policy - Western Contractors Risk & Indemnity Co .docx":

III. Coverage Sections

A. Subcontractor Fraud & Dishonesty Coverage

WCRI will indemnify SBP for financial loss resulting from:

1. Forgery or alteration of inspection documents, certificates, or compliance reports
including structural and weld certifications required for steel beam construction.

2. False or inflated invoicing
including billing for materials not delivered or labor not performed.

3. Intentional concealment of defects or unsafe conditions
such as falsified welding penetration reports or backdated QA/QC logs.

Limit of Liability (A): $5,000,000 aggregate per policy period
Deductible: $25,000 per occurrence

verifier_values.criteria: States that Sweetmarch Build Partners' fraudulent invoicing damages amount to $241,340 (acceptable range $238,927 to $243,753)
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
See "Independent Damages Report - Hired Expert.docx" at page 5:
5.3 Fraudulent Invoicing & Material Overbilling
Overbilled steel vs. delivered (14%): $112,940
Phantom welding hours: $86,100
Fabrication shop surcharges unsupported by logs: $42,300
Subtotal: $241,340
